{"step_by_step_thinking": "Eisenmenger syndrome is characterized by the reversal of a left-to-right shunt to a right-to-left shunt due to severe pulmonary arterial hypertension. This results in dilatation of the central pulmonary arteries and peripheral pruning of the pulmonary arteries. Pulmonary veins are typically distended in Eisenmenger syndrome due to increased blood flow. There is no evidence that RV and LV walls return to normal size in Eisenmenger syndrome.", "answer_choice": "C"}
